input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS (iOS + Android):

---

## 1. Install Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers & tools |
| **.NET SDK (latest)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | .NET runtime + CLI |
| **MAUI workload** | `dotnet workload install maui` | MAUI templates & build targets |

> **Discover versions:** Run `dotnet --list-sdks` and check [the .NET MAUI support policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy/maui) for which .NET SDK versions support MAUI.

---

## 2. Install Order

```bash
# 1 — Install Xcode from the App Store, then launch it once to accept the license
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

# 2 — Command-line tools (may already be installed with Xcode)
xcode-select --install

# 3 — Install .NET SDK (use the installer pkg from dot.net, or Homebrew)
brew install --cask dotnet-sdk   # alternative: download .pkg from dot.net

# 4 — Install the MAUI workload
dotnet workload install maui

# 5 — Android SDK (installed automatically with the MAUI workload,
#      but you can also manage it via Android Studio)
#      Optional: install Android Studio from https://developer.android.com/studio
```

---

## 3. Android-Specific Setup

The MAUI workload bundles the Android SDK & emulator. If you need more control:

- Install **Android Studio** → SDK Manager → install the API level(s) you need.
- Or use the CLI: `dotnet android sdk install` (provided by the workload).
- Accept Android licenses: `dotnet android sdk accept-licenses` (or via Android Studio).
- Create an emulator: Android Studio → Device Manager → create an AVD.

---

## 4. Verify the Installation

```bash
# Check .NET + workloads
dotnet --info
dotnet workload list          # should show "maui" installed

# Check Xcode
xcode-select -p               # should print /Applications/Xcode.app/...
xcrun simctl list devices      # lists available iOS simulators

# Create & build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (simulator)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios \
  -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Build for Android (emulator)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android
```

> Replace `net9.0` with your installed target framework (check `dotnet --list-sdks`).

---

## 5. Optional but Recommended

| Tool | Why |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code + C# Dev Kit** | Lightweight IDE with MAUI support |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** *(discontinued — use VS Code)* | Legacy option |
| **JetBrains Rider** | Full-featured alternative IDE |
| **.NET MAUI Check tool** | `dotnet tool install -g Redth.Net.Maui.Check && maui-check` — audits your environment and fixes issues automatically |

---

**Key gotcha:** Always run `dotnet workload update` periodically — MAUI workloads must match your SDK version.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI development on macOS targeting iOS and Android. This is a knowledge/guidance question — no code changes or repo exploration needed. I'll provide a concise, authoritative answer based on my knowledge.
